journal_entry.reason: Record purchase of delivery vehicle for $40,000: $20,000 paid in cash and $20,000 financed via 2-month promissory note

output_debit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines acquired a delivery vehicle (depreciable property, plant and equipment) for $40,000. The company paid 50% ($20,000) in cash immediately and financed the remaining 50% ($20,000) by issuing a 2-month promissory note. This is a capital asset acquisition with split financing. The vehicle is recorded at its full cost of $40,000 as Motor vehicles (a tangible asset account). Cash is reduced by the amount paid, and a short-term liability (Notes payable) is created for the financed portion. No tax was mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are added.
